video:video.video:title: Hungary's next health minister dances outside parliament after Magyar sworn in as PM
video:video.video:description: Hungarian Health Minister-designate Zsolt Hegedűs danced in front of Budapest's Parliament building after the inauguration of the country’s new national assembly and Péter Magyar as the new prime minister.

video:video.video:title: Fuel shortages and poor quality gasoline drive a surge in electric cars in Bolivia
video:video.video:description: Tired of gasoline shortages and skyrocketing prices, Simón Huanca took matters into his own hands. The 53-year-old Indigenous artisan imported a Chinese electric car to navigate El Alto, Bolivia’s highest city, using the vehicle to transport both his family and the alpaca wool for his weaving worksh

video:video.video:title: Israeli court extends detention of two Gaza aid flotilla activists by two days
video:video.video:description: Spain’s foreign minister on Saturday called for the immediate release of Saif Abukeshek, a dual Spanish-Swedish citizen of Palestinian origin, who was detained for questioning in Israel on Saturday along with Thiago Ávila of Brazil.

video:video.video:title: Activistas: Israel interceptó su flotilla de ayuda a Gaza cerca de Creta y detuvo a tripulantes
video:video.video:description: Activistas: Israel interceptó su flotilla de ayuda a Gaza cerca de Creta y detuvo a tripulantes

video:video.video:title: View from onboard vessel taking passengers from hantavirus-stricken cruise ship to Tenerife port
video:video.video:description: Small vessels were seen carrying people from the MV Hondius to the quayside at Granadilla port. Officials were waiting there to guide them to buses that would transfer them to a medical facility for quarantine.

video:video.video:title: UK army medics parachute onto remote island for suspected hantavirus case
video:video.video:description: British Army medics have parachuted onto the remote South Atlantic territory of Tristan da Cunha, where one of the 221 residents has a suspected case of hantavirus.

video:video.video:title: Putin: Russia's conflict with Ukraine 'coming to an end'
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said a U.S.-brokered ceasefire with Ukraine had emerged following discussions with the United States, following the Russian Ministry of Defense's statement about the possibility of retaliatory attacks on Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 10th: Passengers of a Hantavirus-hit cruise ship are boarding flights back to their home countries; Iran sent response to latest U.S. ceasefire proposal via Pakistani mediators; Russia accuses Kyiv of breaking weekend ceasefire; Wild mountain bongos are being reintr

video:video.video:title: Pediatricians say schools need to make time for recess for kids of all ages
video:video.video:description: For the first time in more than a decade, a leading pediatricians group has put out new guidance about recess, saying it’s crucial for good health and good grades.

video:video.video:title: Iran war disruptions spark higher costs and lost income in Bangladesh
video:video.video:description: Tariqul Islam lost his savings after setbacks in his clothing business about a year and a half ago and turned to ride-sharing on his motorbike to make ends meet.

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma civil rights attorney: Tulsa Race Massacre reparations is soul-redeeming work for America
video:video.video:description: Civil rights attorney Damario Solomon-Simmons learned about the Tulsa Race Massacre during college. This revelation inspired him to lead a reparations campaign for survivors and descendants.

video:video.video:title: AI agent given the chance to manage a cafe in Stockholm, but it's still fine tuning the bread order
video:video.video:description: The coffee might be poured by a human hand, but behind the counter something far less traditional is calling the shots at a new experimental cafe in Stockholm.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainians seeking cultural escape from war’s brutality find comfort and resilience at Kyiv art fair
video:video.video:description: A contemporary art fair was held in the Ukrainian capital with an unusual premise: that art can help a society come to terms with what war has made normal.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is more than ready for the Eurovision stage
video:video.video:description: Australia is fielding "Born to Try" star Delta Goodrem as its entry for the 70th Eurovision Song Contest, where she will compete with "Eclipse." (May 11)

video:video.video:title: Mother's Day traditions stay strong as gas prices rise, but summer vacations are another story
video:video.video:description: Gas prices have risen across the Silver State, as it has across the country, but moms say their Mother's Day plans have gone unchanged.

video:video.video:title: Sticker fever returns to Argentina ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup
video:video.video:description: A month before the start of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, thousands of Argentines are already living the tournament through the country’s tradition of collecting and trading soccer stickers.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart at his roast: 'There's only one me'
video:video.video:description: Kevin Hart promises to return fire at comics who insult him at Netflix's "The Roast of Kevin Hart." (May 10)

video:video.video:title: Thailand's controversial former leader Thaksin Shinawatra released after eight months in prison
video:video.video:description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra, whose 21st-century political odyssey sharply divided Thai society for decades, was released from a Bangkok prison on Monday after serving eight months of a one-year sentence for a corruption-related charge.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson makes rare appearance at Grammy Hall of Fame Gala
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson made a rare appearance at the 2026 Grammy Hall of Fame Gala, where her album "Rhythm Nation 1814" was inducted.

video:video.video:title: 'A moment of sacrifices': Italian farmers wrestle with economic fallout of Iran war
video:video.video:description: Since Israel and U.S. attacked Iran on Feb. 28, diesel for agricultural vehicles in Italy has risen from 0.85 euros ($1) per litre to nearly 1.50 ($1.76), while fertilizer ingredients have gone up by 35%.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister vows to prove 'doubters' wrong after election losses
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer made the pledge Monday, referencing members of his own party and among the electorate as a whole wrong as he tries to fight off demands to step down after devastating local election results for his Labour Party.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 11
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, May 11th: Americans evacuated from ship due to hantavirus flown to Nebraska; Trump rejects Iran’s latest response to U.S. proposal; Oil prices rise; Israel says it blew up tunnels in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart stays fearless in the face of his own roast
video:video.video:description: Comedy superstar Kevin Hart tells his roasters - including Deon Cole, Mo Amer, Tom Segura - to "have at it," as they arrive for the live taping of Netlfix's "The Roast of Kevin Hart."

video:video.video:title: How evacuation of hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius unfolded in Tenerife
video:video.video:description: The first plane carrying passengers from the cruise ship left Spain’s Canary Islands on Sunday for Madrid, where they will be taken to a military hospital.

video:video.video:title: Trump-Xi summit has high stakes for Taiwan
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has demonstrated greater ambivalence toward Taiwan in his second term. That's raising questions about his commitment to maintaining American support for the self-ruled island ahead of next week's summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Plane carrying passengers from hantavirus ship lands in Nebraska
video:video.video:description: A plane carrying American passengers, who were evacuated from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship, landed in Omaha, Nebraska early Monday.

video:video.video:title: Lox Pratt says it's good to be bad
video:video.video:description: At age 14, actor Lox Pratt has already taken on two major bad guy roles in a row - as Jack in Netflix mini-series "Lord of the Files" and Draco Malfoy in the upcoming "Harry Potter" TV show.

video:video.video:title: Captain of hantavirus-stricken ship thanks crew and passengers for their 'unity' and 'strength'
video:video.video:description: The captain of the hantavirus-stricken ship thanked his crew and passengers for their 'unity' and 'strength' in a video message released on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan Taliban splinter group claims responsibility for deadly attack on security post
video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber and several gunmen detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near the post in Bannu, a district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan, late Saturday, police said.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision stage ready, or stage fright?
video:video.video:description: Contestants reveal how they're feeling about performing at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest.

video:video.video:title: Iran spokesman says Tehran doesn't care if US not happy with response to peace proposal
video:video.video:description: Iran sent its response to the latest U.S. proposal to end the Iran war via Pakistani mediators on Sunday, but U.S.

video:video.video:title: Christian infertility doctor confronts mounting ethical dilemmas in modern IVF
video:video.video:description: After decades in fertility care, Dr. John Gordon launched a new clinic to address ethical concerns in IVF, like surplus embryos and genetic screening.

video:video.video:title: Fab Five alum, high school founder Jalen Rose has ‘Mama, I made it’ moment as commencement speaker
video:video.video:description: Michigan Fab Five member and Detroit high school founder Jalen Rose has his ‘Mama, I made it’ moment as the commencement speaker at the University of Michigan in Ann Arbor.

video:video.video:title: Widow of FSU shooting victim is suing OpenAI, attorney calls ChatGPT a 'co-conspirator'
video:video.video:description: The lawsuit claims that Phoenix Ikner, the alleged gunman who was a 20-year-old FSU student at the time, used ChatGPT for "input and assistance" to carry-out the shooting that killed two people and wounded six others.

video:video.video:title: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship arrive in Nebraska and Georgia
video:video.video:description: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius arrived in Nebraska and Georgia, where they will receive care and monitoring at medical facilities.
video:video.video:tag: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

video:video.video:title: Watch a dramatic thunderstorm sweep across northwestern Kansas
video:video.video:description: Timelapse footage shows a thunderstorm as it sweeps across northwestern Kansas.

video:video.video:title: Lights, camera, selfie - contestants arrive for Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Artists from 35 countries are competing to win the Eurovision Song Contest, which culminates with a Grand Final in Vienna on Saturday. (May 11)

video:video.video:title: Iran and US at impasse and ceasefire shaky ahead of Trump's visit to China
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to use a trip this week to China to urge Chinese President Xi Jinping to pressure Iran into making concessions and end the limbo.

video:video.video:title: ‘KENREX’ turns a true crime into a one-man theatrical thriller
video:video.video:description: In search of the right true crime story, actor-writer Jack Holden says the 1981 Missouri killing of Ken Rex McElroy leant itself to a stage performance.

video:video.video:title: Man charged in White House correspondents' dinner attack pleads not guilty
video:video.video:description: A California man accused of storming the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner while armed with guns and knives has pleaded not guilty to charges he attempted to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Why many adopted kids end up in residential treatment centers, an AP investigation
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation finds a business known for tough-love boarding schools for rebellious teenagers has set its sights on a different demographic: adopted kids.

video:video.video:title: Heat stroke is suspected among 6 found dead in a shipping container at a rail yard near Texas border
video:video.video:description: A South Texas medical examiner believes heat stroke may have led to the death of six people thought to be immigrants who were found Sunday afternoon inside a shipping container at a Union Pacific rail yard near the Mexico border in Laredo, Texas.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 11th: President Trump wants to suspend the federal tax on gas; Hantavirus exposed cruise ship passengers fly home; Heat stroke may have killed six people by Texas border; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ziggy Marley on legacy, philanthropy and new album | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ziggy Marley is looking to the "Brightside." The Grammy-winning 57-year-old musician focused his ninth solo studio album on optimism and mental health, along with nods to legacy.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart shares 'message to all'
video:video.video:description: Kevin Hart shares a "message to all" at his live Netflix roast that featured stars like Tom Brady, Usher, Chelsea Handler and more.

video:video.video:title: Deon Cole reflects on Altadena benefit show
video:video.video:description: Deon Cole reflects on hosting a series of Netflix-sponsored stand-up comedy shows in Altadena as the community rebuilds after last year's deadly Eaton Fire.

video:video.video:title: Trump celebrates the national champion Indiana Hoosiers at the White House
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump welcomed the 2026 national champion Indiana Hoosiers to the White House Monday, praising their undefeated 16-0 season.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's LELÉKA connects with fans ahead of Eurovision's semifinal
video:video.video:description: Eurovision contestant LELÉKA met with members of her native Ukrainian community in Vienna to connect with fans.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus patients arrive at Emory University Hospital
video:video.video:description: Two American passengers, arrived Monday to Emory University Hospital in Atlanta after being taken off the MVS-Hondius cruise ship that was hit with a hantavirus outbreak.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: President Macron tells off noisy audience during presentation at Nairobi university event
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron gave a telling off to a noisy audience during a presentation at the University of Nairobi.

video:video.video:title: Ziggy Marley says following his father's legacy is 'organic'
video:video.video:description: Ziggy Marley says upholding his father Bob Marley's legacy comes naturally. His new album is “Brightside.”

video:video.video:title: New wave of gang violence in Haiti displaces hundreds of people
video:video.video:description: A new wave of gang violence in Haiti’s capital forced hundreds to flee their homes over the weekend, leaving families scattered along the road to the country’s main airport on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine forces join NATO counterparts for specialised drone training on Sweden's Gotland
video:video.video:description: Swedish, American and Ukrainian forces are taking part in Swedish-led military exercises on the Baltic island of Gotland.

video:video.video:title: Final evacuations of people from hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife wrap up
video:video.video:description: The last remaining passengers on a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak disembarked Monday and boarded flights to more than 20 countries to enter quarantine.

video:video.video:title: Trump says ceasefire is on 'life support' after rejecting Iran's latest proposal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the Iran ceasefire is on “life support” after he rejected the country’s latest proposal for not including a nuclear concession.

video:video.video:title: Iranian news agency posts new video said to show still closed Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Fars news agency posted a video on Monday showing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. According to a Fars correspondent, the video shows ships in the northern part of the strait, which is still closed.

video:video.video:title: Iran war adds tension to Trump-Xi summit in China
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is heading back to China, but this time, the Iran war raises the stakes for both sides. China is the bigger purchaser of Iranian oil.

video:video.video:title: Israeli settlers force Palestinian man to exhume his father's body and rebury him
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian family's efforts to bury 80-year-old Hussein Asasa in a cemetery in the northern West Bank were disrupted by Israelis from a nearby recently legalized settlement, according to family members and footage of the incident obtained by The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Emmanuel Macron joins two-time Olympic champion Eliud Kipchoge for morning run in Nairobi
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron joined legendary Kenyan marathon runner Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning jog in Nairobi on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Expert: Federal gas tax brings benefits; worries suspension could become permanent
video:video.video:description: President Trump proposed suspending the federal gas tax that funds highway infrastructure to ease spiking prices at the pump.

video:video.video:title: IDL brings global crews together in high-stakes battle
video:video.video:description: Connor Lim discusses his International Dance League, which brings together global dance crews in high-stakes battles. (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Iran war, Taiwan and unresolved trade tensions all likely to feature on agenda for Trump-Xi meeting
video:video.video:description: Few expect major breakthroughs to the long-running frictions between China and the U.S., which range from competition in technology to the thorny question of Taiwan, whose main ally is the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Chinese people share mixed feelings ahead of Trump's visit to China
video:video.video:description: Chinese people shared mixed feelings and expectations on Monday ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's meeting with President Xi Jinping.
